q: Carbon is the main constituent of fossil fuels like coal, oil, and natural gas.
A large amount of electrical energy is directly stored inside raw fossil fuels.
Fossil fuels are excellent sources of energy for our transportation needs.
The fossil fuel industry extracts crude oil from the ground and converts it to gasoline.
Fossil fuel liquids are refined into petrochemical products that serve as ingredients for manufacturing plastics and polyurethane.
Coal is formed in a process called coalification.
Coal is a perfectly uniform substance whose chemical composition remains completely identical from deposit to deposit.
Coal is made of decomposed plant matter in conditions of high temperature and pressure.
Natural gas takes nearly as long to form as coal and oil deposits do.
Methane is a common gas found in swamps.
Methane is also a natural by-product of the digestive system of animals.
Nuclear energy is classified as a renewable energy source because uranium fuels are easily replenished during operation.
Uranium fuels used in nuclear energy are consumed in a nuclear fission reaction.
Vessels driven by nuclear energy must frequently stop to refuel during short-range voyages.
Radioisotopes obtained as by-products in nuclear reactions are actively used in medicine, agriculture, and research.
